title: Module 3 quiz: Scheduled and background tasks

questions.prompt: Daily digest runs every morning but most days have nothing notable. Mute rate is rising. Fix?

choices.text: Add more content to every day's digest.

Skip days with nothing notable. Silence is information — users learn to read when the digest arrives because the assistant filtered. Add per-task disable, vacation pause, day-of-week overrides for full control.
Use Opus to write fancier prose.

questions.explanation: Forcing daily output trains users to ignore. Skipping when nothing's notable trains them to attend. Embrace silence; surface only when there's signal.

choices.text: Pause and disable controls: per-task pause, vacation mode, quiet hours, day-of-week overrides. Users need to halt scheduled work instantly when life happens. Without it, retention drops during every vacation and busy week.

questions.explanation: Pause controls are some of the most-used assistant features once they exist. First-class, not v2-maybe.

questions.prompt: Webhook storm fires dozens of unrelated events. Without protections, what happens?

choices.text: Notification flood. Defenses: per-event circuit breakers (stop firing after N events/min from a source), per-user rate limits, dedup keys, engineering alerts on storm patterns. One upstream incident can otherwise mute the assistant for every user.

questions.explanation: Event firehoses are real. Circuit breakers + rate limits + dedup + alerting. Catch storms before user trust erodes.

questions.prompt: When an event-triggered notification fires, what should always be shown?

choices.text: What triggered it: 'Triggered by: new email from Sarah (VIP rule)' or 'Triggered by: calendar conflict detected'. Users distrust unexplained interruptions; surfacing the trigger ties the alert to a rule they understand and can edit.

questions.explanation: Trigger transparency builds trust over time. Users learn 'why this fired' — they can adjust rules, ignore intentionally, or appreciate the catch.

questions.prompt: What's the right default delivery channel for a new assistant notification?

choices.text: In-app inbox or daily digest email. Push is precious; reserve for things the user explicitly told you matter (VIP emails, security, on-call). Default conservative; let users tune up specific rules.

questions.explanation: User attention is finite. Default to less-urgent surfaces; the user opts up the rules they actually care about. Push-by-default leads to fatigue and mute.
